sub_instance.problem_statement:
Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape, Harbor, Grove, Ridge, Cove

Required terminals:
- Port, Rock, Cape

- Bay-Mill: cost 15

- Mill-Rock: cost 13
- Mill-Ridge: cost 6
- Cape-Cliff: cost 4
- Cape-Dune: cost 14
- Cape-Mill: cost 8

- Harbor-Port: cost 3

- Bay / Harbor
- Dune / Pine
- Dune / Rock
- Harbor / Port
- Cove / Port
- Cove / Rock
- Cove / Dune
- Cove / Harbor

- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA2 (7) -> MA5 (7) -> MA1 (1) -> MA3 (8) -> MA6 (5) -> MA7 (4) -> MA4 (2); Factory B MB4 (6) -> MB6 (1) -> MB7 (1) -> MB2 (1) -> MB1 (9) -> MB5 (1) -> MB3 (7)
- Job 2: Factory A MA6 (5) -> MA2 (1) -> MA4 (7) -> MA1 (9) -> MA3 (2) -> MA5 (3) -> MA7 (5); Factory B MB4 (2) -> MB5 (6) -> MB2 (9) -> MB3 (7) -> MB1 (9) -> MB7 (4) -> MB6 (5)
- Job 3: Factory A MA3 (9) -> MA5 (6) -> MA4 (2) -> MA6 (8) -> MA7 (9) -> MA1 (2) -> MA2 (3); Factory B MB2 (9) -> MB6 (7) -> MB4 (6) -> MB5 (8) -> MB3 (1) -> MB1 (8) -> MB7 (1)
- Job 4: Factory A MA3 (9) -> MA6 (6) -> MA5 (6) -> MA4 (8) -> MA7 (5) -> MA1 (9) -> MA2 (1); Factory B MB5 (7) -> MB2 (9) -> MB1 (3) -> MB6 (9) -> MB3 (9) -> MB7 (4) -> MB4 (7)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (1) -> MA4 (4) -> MA3 (3) -> MA2 (9) -> MA5 (3) -> MA6 (2) -> MA7 (9); Factory B MB7 (5) -> MB3 (1) -> MB4 (2) -> MB6 (2) -> MB1 (1) -> MB2 (8) -> MB5 (1)
- Job 6: Factory A MA7 (5) -> MA3 (8) -> MA2 (6) -> MA6 (8) -> MA1 (8) -> MA4 (2) -> MA5 (1); Factory B MB3 (5) -> MB1 (7) -> MB2 (6) -> MB5 (7) -> MB4 (4) -> MB6 (5) -> MB7 (2)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 216
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[14-15],J2[41-50],J3[86-88],J4[102-111],J5[111-112],J6[169-177]; MA2: J1[0-7],J2[33-34],J3[88-91],J4[111-112],J5[119-128],J6[155-161]; MA3: J1[15-23],J2[50-52],J3[52-61],J4[61-70],J5[116-119],J6[147-155]; MA4: J1[32-34],J2[34-41],J3[67-69],J4[89-97],J5[112-116],J6[177-179]; MA5: J1[7-14],J2[52-55],J3[61-67],J4[83-89],J5[128-131],J6[179-180]; MA6: J1[23-28],J2[28-33],J3[69-77],J4[77-83],J5[131-133],J6[161-169]; MA7: J1[28-32],J2[55-60],J3[77-86],J4[97-102],J5[133-142],J6[142-147]
Factory B: MB1: J1[43-52],J2[84-93],J3[124-132],J4[139-142],J5[175-176],J6[185-192]; MB2: J1[42-43],J2[68-77],J3[91-100],J4[130-139],J5[176-184],J6[192-198]; MB3: J1[53-60],J2[77-84],J3[123-124],J4[151-160],J5[169-170],J6[180-185]; MB4: J1[34-40],J2[60-62],J3[109-115],J4[164-171],J5[171-173],J6[205-209]; MB5: J1[52-53],J2[62-68],J3[115-123],J4[123-130],J5[184-185],J6[198-205]; MB6: J1[40-41],J2[97-102],J3[102-109],J4[142-151],J5[173-175],J6[209-214]; MB7: J1[41-42],J2[93-97],J3[132-133],J4[160-164],J5[164-169],J6[214-216]
Makespan: 216

sub_instance.answer_contract: Object with "factory_a", "factory_b", and "makespan". Each factory maps machine names to lists of ["J<id>", start, end] triples.
sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA5 (2) -> MA1 (4) -> MA4 (6) -> MA7 (1) -> MA3 (7) -> MA6 (3) -> MA2 (6); Factory B MB4 (7) -> MB7 (7) -> MB3 (5) -> MB2 (5) -> MB1 (8) -> MB6 (3) -> MB5 (5)
- Job 2: Factory A MA6 (9) -> MA3 (6) -> MA2 (4) -> MA4 (6) -> MA1 (9) -> MA5 (8) -> MA7 (7); Factory B MB1 (8) -> MB5 (2) -> MB7 (6) -> MB2 (9) -> MB6 (3) -> MB3 (4) -> MB4 (7)
- Job 3: Factory A MA2 (2) -> MA1 (8) -> MA6 (3) -> MA4 (4) -> MA7 (6) -> MA5 (7) -> MA3 (7); Factory B MB2 (8) -> MB4 (4) -> MB5 (5) -> MB3 (3) -> MB6 (9) -> MB1 (3) -> MB7 (2)
- Job 4: Factory A MA3 (6) -> MA4 (6) -> MA7 (4) -> MA2 (2) -> MA5 (5) -> MA1 (8) -> MA6 (7); Factory B MB4 (3) -> MB3 (7) -> MB6 (8) -> MB7 (4) -> MB1 (9) -> MB5 (5) -> MB2 (9)
- Job 5: Factory A MA4 (3) -> MA5 (9) -> MA7 (8) -> MA1 (2) -> MA6 (7) -> MA2 (1) -> MA3 (1); Factory B MB4 (8) -> MB2 (9) -> MB7 (2) -> MB3 (1) -> MB5 (6) -> MB6 (2) -> MB1 (3)
- Job 6: Factory A MA1 (7) -> MA5 (6) -> MA4 (9) -> MA6 (9) -> MA7 (3) -> MA2 (4) -> MA3 (6); Factory B MB7 (1) -> MB5 (4) -> MB4 (8) -> MB3 (1) -> MB6 (2) -> MB2 (5) -> MB1 (7)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 220
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[2-6],J2[48-57],J3[57-65],J4[115-123],J5[132-134],J6[134-141]; MA2: J1[23-29],J2[38-42],J3[42-44],J4[108-110],J5[141-142],J6[168-172]; MA3: J1[13-20],J2[32-38],J3[85-92],J4[92-98],J5[142-143],J6[172-178]; MA4: J1[6-12],J2[42-48],J3[68-72],J4[98-104],J5[104-107],J6[147-156]; MA5: J1[0-2],J2[57-65],J3[78-85],J4[110-115],J5[115-124],J6[141-147]; MA6: J1[20-23],J2[23-32],J3[65-68],J4[123-130],J5[134-141],J6[156-165]; MA7: J1[12-13],J2[65-72],J3[72-78],J4[104-108],J5[124-132],J6[165-168]
Factory B: MB1: J1[53-61],J2[72-80],J3[132-135],J4[152-161],J5[195-198],J6[213-220]; MB2: J1[48-53],J2[88-97],J3[97-105],J4[166-175],J5[175-184],J6[208-213]; MB3: J1[43-48],J2[100-104],J3[120-123],J4[133-140],J5[186-187],J6[205-206]; MB4: J1[29-36],J2[104-111],J3[111-115],J4[130-133],J5[143-151],J6[197-205]; MB5: J1[64-69],J2[80-82],J3[115-120],J4[161-166],J5[187-193],J6[193-197]; MB6: J1[61-64],J2[97-100],J3[123-132],J4[140-148],J5[193-195],J6[206-208]; MB7: J1[36-43],J2[82-88],J3[135-137],J4[148-152],J5[184-186],J6[186-187]
Makespan: 220

sub_instance.problem_statement:
Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape, Harbor, Grove, Ridge, Cove

Required terminals:
- Bay, Dune, Grove

Available cable links:
- Port-Ridge: cost 15
- Bay-Pine: cost 10
- Bay-Rock: cost 13
- Bay-Cape: cost 2
- Bay-Ridge: cost 13
- Cliff-Rock: cost 14
- Cliff-Dune: cost 2
- Cliff-Mill: cost 16
- Cliff-Harbor: cost 2
- Pine-Ridge: cost 6
- Dune-Rock: cost 16
- Dune-Mill: cost 16
- Dune-Harbor: cost 2
- Mill-Port: cost 11
- Mill-Pine: cost 15
- Cape-Pine: cost 13
- Cape-Rock: cost 13
- Cape-Ridge: cost 16
- Harbor-Rock: cost 15
- Harbor-Mill: cost 16
- Grove-Port: cost 8
- Grove-Pine: cost 8
- Grove-Mill: cost 12
- Grove-Ridge: cost 7
- Cove-Port: cost 13
- Cove-Pine: cost 8
- Cove-Grove: cost 6
- Cove-Ridge: cost 3

Interference pairs:
- Bay / Pine
- Bay / Cape
- Mill / Port
- Cape / Pine
- Cape / Grove
- Grove / Port
- Grove / Mill
- Cove / Rock

Rules:
- Your cable choice must be a tree, not a cyclic network.
- All required terminals must lie in one connected component.
- Frequencies matter only for active villages in the chosen network.
- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

Baseline total cost: 77
Baseline answer summary:
Edges: Bay-Pine(10), Bay-Rock(13), Dune-Rock(16), Grove-Pine(8)
Active villages: Bay, Pine, Rock, Dune, Grove
Frequencies: Bay=1, Pine=2, Rock=1, Dune=1, Grove=1
Edge cost: 47
Frequencies used: 2
Total cost: 77

Answer contract hint: Object with "edges": [["Port", "Bay"], ...] and "frequencies": {"Port": 1, ...}.
